Guys, welcome to module three, the segmentation and confirmation phase. And before we get into this particular module, I just want to make it quick. No, because this really is the first of the five modules where we're going to be digging into these sub sequences and exactly how to go about them and really absorb the information but also apply it right away, you know, and really act upon it to create those emails that go into your funnel or your sequences. So this is how these models are going to go on from here on out. We're going to start off with the main lesson video where you're really going to be getting that full rundown of what we're coaching in this sequence. So you're going to get that deep dive of exactly what this part of your funnel is selling or coaching and some tips and strategies on how to do that. Then we're going to hop into the workbooks and you're going to get a rundown of those major points from the lesson, you know, the really key takeaways that I want to be super fresh in your mind as you approach crafting your messaging for this. And then we're going to go over those key questions to ask, you know, direct response 2.0 style and some of them, most of them actually will be very familiar from the 47 questions of workbook. So if you did the work back in model two, that's a great, you're going to be prepared to really storm through this aspect of the funnel. If not, this is where you are going to have to sit down and just answer and explore these questions. And I just don't want you to rush it, you know, just spend an extra 30 seconds on it. If you think you're done, just just sit in silence with it and see what else comes up, you know, be patient. Patience is a competitive advantage sometimes to just be willing to explore those questions as fully as you can and then going even a little bit beyond that. And then finally also in the workbooks, you're going to be getting the theme plates and the examples. So these are going to be the emails I go to, to activate those things for coaching. You know, you're going to be leveraging a lot of the information you uncover in those questions and using that to execute these theme plates. But these theme plates are designed to really be a container for coaching that specific belief, behavior, action or trigger that is crucial for that aspect of your funnel. So that's how we are going to approach modules three through seven. And you're going to get those theme plate walkthroughs, but there's one thing I want to touch upon one final time. There's just no such thing as swipe and deploy. I mean, that sounds like a warfare strategy that you'd hear in Star Trek. You know, it has no place in marketing. It has no place in connecting with your audience in a genuine way and actually trying to coach that transformation. So we're not swiping and deploying these are theme plates. They are loose containers, but they're definitely here to guide your thinking and just take a lot of the guesswork out of writing these emails. You know, if there's one thing I've noticed, it's that people aren't afraid to roll up their sleeves, sit down and do the work. They just want that confidence to double down on doing it properly. You know, no one wants to be wasting their time. So that's what these containers are designed for, to make you feel like you are putting that effort and energy and applying it in a way that actually is going to have a payoff. So that's what we're doing here, not swipe and deploy. That's why you're not getting quote unquote swipe files and like ad lib, mad lib, ad lib or mad lib. I think it's mad lib, you know, mad lib style things where you just fill in the blank, you know, that connects with absolutely nobody and you probably don't feel good sending that out. So no such thing as swipe and deploy, but use these as containers, use the guiding questions and then really just kind of let it naturally flow from there. So that's what I'm going to leave you with before we go into module three, which is the segmentation and confirmation. It's really the start of this journey and I can't wait to see you in the main lesson. So I'll catch you there in a sec.
